places.name: Santa Maria della Concezione Crypts
places.description: The crypts of Capuchin friars decorated with the bones of over 4,000 friars, including an entire "crypt of pelvises."

places.name: Torre Argentina (Roman Cat Sanctuary)
places.description: Hundreds of lucky felines haunt the ruins where Caesar was murdered.

places.name: Galleria Sciarra
places.description: This hidden Art Nouveau courtyard is an opulent feast for the eyes.

places.name: The Dome Illusion
places.description: A master of trompe-l'oeil creates a stunning, tourist-fooling dome out of a flat ceiling.

places.name: The Aventine Keyhole
places.description: Rome's semi-secret peephole vista is also a former Crusader stronghold.

places.name: The Mouth of Truth
places.description: The yawning maw of this pagan visage is said to bite off the hands of liars.

places.name: Pasticceria Boccione
places.description: In Rome’s Jewish ghetto, a 200-year-old bakery still makes the neighborhood’s historic sweets.

places.name: Servian Wall at McDonald's
places.description: Inside this McDonald's you can dine on your Big Mac and fries next to a 2,500-year-old Roman wall.

places.name: Garum Biblioteca e Museo Della Cucina
places.description: Rome is now home to a stunning food museum and library.
